title: Transforming yeast
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"chk8l","text":"In the same way that we transformed E. coli by making them take up a plasmid, we will transform yeast by inducing them to take up both a plasmid (to cut your genomic location) and some linear DNA to repair that location.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3bud3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3xw7h","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ekabg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"image","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"63A37A02341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","height":803,"is_video":false,"legend":"{\"blocks\":null,\"entityMap\":null}","mime":"image/png","original_name":"full knockout.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/files/iutkbvh6p.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/iutibvh6p.jpg","type":1,"webp_placeholder":"https://s3.amazonaws.com/protocols-files/files/iutjbvh6p.webp","webp_source":"https://content.protocols.io/files/iuthbvh6p.webp","width":550}}}}

steps: Compute the volume of your Cas9 plasmid that contains 1 µg of DNA. Compute the volume of your URA3 PCR product that contains 500 ng of DNA. If you don't have enough DNA for one or both of these, check with an instructor.
Immediately upon retrieving a tube of yeast cells from the -80 °C freezer, thaw them rapidly by putting them in the 42 °C water bath for 00:00:30
Centrifuge 13.000 x g, , 2 minutes . Remove and discard the supernatant.

Add the following to the tube of yeast cells in order: 260 µL Polyethylene Glycol (PEG) 3350 Electron Microscopy Sciences Catalog #19760 50 Mass / % volume 36 µL Lithium Acetate Dihydrate Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #L4158 1 Molarity (M) 50 µL Salmon Sperm DNA Research Products International Corp (RPI) Catalog #D52150 2 mg/mL Enough plasmid DNA to equal 1 µg of DNA (computed above) Enough PCR product to equal 500 ng of DNA (computed above)
Vortex vigorously to resuspend the pellet in the transformation mix. (This may take from 30 seconds to a minute -- be patient!
Incubate in the 42 °C water bath for 00:30:00 Use a water bath float so you don't have to stand there!
Centrifuge 13000 x g, °C, 30 seconds . Aspirate the supernatant.
Pipette 1 mL of sterile water into the transformation tube. Stir the pellet with a micropipette tip to break up the cell pellet, then vortex to thoroughly resuspend the pellet.
Pour 5-10 glass beads on a Uracil dropout plate. Pipette 100 µL of cells onto it. Shake the plate to spread the cells out.
Pour 5-10 glass beads on a Leucine plate. Pipette 100 µL of cells onto it. Shake the plate to spread the cells out.
creator: Brian Teague
